description: When planning/proving a formalisation, never deviate from the source. Decompose by TRANSCRIBING the source's actual proof, not by inventing a route that 'looks provable in Lean' or 'connects to existing code'. Every leaf cites a precise source locator (statement number + page + line); a leaf you cannot quote verbatim is an ARTIFACT - find the source's real route. Tells you've left the source: needing substantial mathlib-lacking infrastructure, or a leaf turning out false / true-only-under-an-extra-hypothesis the source never uses. Case: 'O_X(U)=A<T/s> strongly noetherian' is one line in Wedhorn Example 6.38 (A->A<T/s> is topologically of finite type; t.f.t. over a strongly-noetherian Tate ring is strongly noetherian, Remark 6.37(1)). An invented route (quotient + restricted-power-series Fubini + Banach-OMT) was a pure artifact costing a multi-hour session; the Fubini isn't in mathlib. Baked into /develop as the 'Source-faithfulness (BINDING)' section.
